title: EoSB1 Process for America's App - Android
summary: End-of-Sprint Build process for America's Android App. Covers build generation, QA handoff, pilot branch strategy and release coordination.
content:
## Overview

The EoSB1 (End of Sprint Build 1) process defines how the Android team generates, validates and hands off the sprint build for America's App.

## Steps

1. Merge all approved PRs to develop
2. Run updateconfig.py to bump version codes
3. Trigger GitHub Actions build workflow
4. Upload artifact to Azure DevOps
5. Notify QA team via Microsoft Teams
6. Create pilot branch (extra/pilot-x.xx.x)
7. Run Lokalise string export
8. Run smoke tests on pilot build
9. Handoff to QA for full regression

## Version Management

Version codes are managed via updateconfig.py. Changes to build.gradle or build.gradle.kts require manual review before build.

## Release Coordination

Release notes coordinated through Azure DevOps tickets. Pilot branch strategy follows extra/pilot-x.xx.x naming.
aiUpdateSignals: Changes in QA smoke test process
openAIQuestions: Has the pilot branch naming convention changed in the latest sprint?
Are there new steps in the QA smoke test checklist?
suggestedActions: Verify GitHub Actions workflow is up to date
Confirm QA handoff process with team
mermaidDiagram:
flowchart TD
    A[Merge PRs to develop] --> B[Run updateconfig.py]
    B --> C[Trigger GitHub Actions]
    C --> D[Upload to Azure DevOps]
    D --> E[Notify QA via Teams]
    E --> F[Create pilot branch]
    F --> G[Run Lokalise export]
    G --> H[Run smoke tests]
    H --> I[QA Full Regression]

title: Authentication Flow - Android
summary: Describes the authentication flow for the Android Shell App, including token management, refresh logic and secure storage via Azure Secrets and Keychain.
content:
## Overview

The Android authentication flow handles user login, token acquisition, refresh cycles and secure storage.

## Token Flow

1. User initiates login
2. App requests auth token from identity provider
3. Token stored securely via Android Keystore
4. Refresh token lifecycle managed by AuthRepository
5. On expiry, silent refresh attempted
6. On failure, user redirected to login

## Secrets Management

Client credentials are stored in Azure Secrets. Never hardcoded in source. Retrieved at build time via GitHub Actions secrets injection.
suggestedActions: Verify token expiry times match current identity provider config
Check Azure Secrets for updated client credentials
mermaidDiagram:
flowchart TD
    A[User Login] --> B[Request Auth Token]
    B --> C[Store in Keystore]
    C --> D[Token Valid?]
    D -- Yes --> E[Proceed]
    D -- No --> F[Silent Refresh]
    F -- Success --> E
    F -- Fail --> G[Redirect to Login]

title: Rewards Integration Overview
summary: Architecture overview of the Shell Rewards integration, covering points accumulation, redemption flows and the Shell Go+ loyalty program connection.
content:
## Overview

The Rewards integration connects the Shell App to the loyalty backend, enabling points accumulation at fuel transactions and redemption via the Shell Go+ program.

## Points Accumulation

- Triggered on successful fuel transaction
- Points calculated server-side
- Result returned in transaction confirmation payload

## Redemption Flow

1. User selects reward in app
2. App sends redemption request to Rewards API
3. Server validates points balance
4. Fuel discount applied to transaction
5. Points deducted from balance

## Shell Go+ Integration

Shell Go+ members receive enhanced rewards multipliers. Integration uses a dedicated loyalty endpoint.
aiUpdateSignals: Changes in Shell Go+ loyalty rules
openAIQuestions: Has the Shell Go+ multiplier logic changed in the latest rewards SDK?
Are there new redemption categories not reflected here?
suggestedActions: Verify redemption flow with current Rewards API version

title: Branch Deep Link Handling
summary: Guide for implementing and testing deep link handling in the Shell App using the Branch SDK, covering URI schemes, App Links, Universal Links and deferred deep linking.
content:
## Overview

Deep links allow external sources to open specific in-app content. The Shell App uses Branch SDK for attribution and deferred deep linking.

## Android Implementation

- App Links configured via Digital Asset Links JSON
- Intent filters registered in AndroidManifest.xml
- Branch SDK initialized in Application class

## iOS Implementation

- Universal Links configured via apple-app-site-association
- Associated Domains entitlement required
- Branch SDK handles link routing

## Testing

Use the Branch dashboard to create test links. Verify with adb on Android and xcrun simctl on iOS.

title: Lokalise Strings Update Process
summary: Step-by-step process for updating and exporting localization strings via Lokalise for both Android and iOS, including the GitHub Actions automation pipeline.
content:
## Overview

All user-facing strings are managed in Lokalise. This process covers how to add new strings, request translations and export them to the codebase.

## Adding New Strings

1. Add string key and source text in Lokalise dashboard
2. Assign to correct project (Android or iOS)
3. Request translation via Lokalise workflow
4. Wait for translation approval

## Exporting Strings

1. Trigger lokalise-export GitHub Actions workflow
2. Workflow pulls approved translations from Lokalise API
3. Generates strings.xml (Android) or Localizable.strings (iOS)
4. Opens PR with updated string files
5. Review and merge PR

## Automation

The export is also triggered automatically on EoSB1 build day via the main build workflow.
aiUpdateSignals: Changes in Lokalise API token configuration
openAIQuestions: Has the Lokalise export workflow been updated to support plurals?
suggestedActions: Verify Lokalise API token is still valid
Check if any new string projects were added
mermaidDiagram:
flowchart TD
    A[Add string in Lokalise] --> B[Request translation]
    B --> C[Translation approved]
    C --> D[Trigger export workflow]
    D --> E[Generate string files]
    E --> F[Open PR]
    F --> G[Merge to develop]

title: Azure Secrets Management for Mobile
summary: Guide for managing mobile app secrets in Azure Key Vault, including secret naming conventions, GitHub Actions injection patterns and rotation procedures.
content:
## Overview

All mobile app secrets (API keys, client credentials, signing certificates) are stored in Azure Key Vault. Never hardcode secrets in source.

## Secret Naming Convention

- Android: ANDROID_{ENVIRONMENT}_{SECRET_NAME}
- iOS: IOS_{ENVIRONMENT}_{SECRET_NAME}
- Shared: MOBILE_{ENVIRONMENT}_{SECRET_NAME}

## GitHub Actions Injection

Secrets are injected at build time via environment variables in GitHub Actions workflows. Use az keyvault secret show or the Azure Login action.

## Rotation Procedure

1. Create new secret version in Azure Portal
2. Update GitHub Actions environment variables
3. Trigger test build to verify
4. Revoke old secret version after 7 days

## Keychain Storage

Runtime secrets (user tokens, session keys) are stored in the device Keychain/Android Keystore.
aiUpdateSignals: Changes in GitHub Actions secrets configuration
suggestedActions: Verify all secrets listed are still active in Azure Key Vault
Check rotation schedule for expiring secrets
